mission.title: Install and Configure Kudo on Kubernetes
mission.description: KUDO (Kubernetes Universal Declarative Operator) provides a declarative approach to building production-grade Kubernetes Operators, covering the entire application lifecycle. Installing KUDO allows you to manage your applications on Kubernetes more effectively by leveraging its operator framework.

steps.title: Add the KUDO Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the KUDO Helm chart repository to your local Helm setup with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kudo https://mesosphere.github.io/charts/staging
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install KUDO using Helm
steps.description:
Install KUDO in the `kudo-system` namespace with the following command:
```bash
helm install kudo kudo/kudo --namespace kudo-system --create-namespace --version v0.19.0
```

steps.title: Verify KUDO installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the KUDO pods to ensure they are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kudo-system
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for KUDO
steps.description:
To set resource limits for KUDO, edit the Helm release:
```bash
helm upgrade kudo kudo/kudo --namespace kudo-system --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for KUDO
steps.description:
Create a ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding for KUDO:
```bash
kubectl create clusterrole kudo-role --verb=* --resource=* 

kubectl create clusterrolebinding kudo-role-binding --clusterrole=kudo-role --serviceaccount=kudo-system:kudo
```

uninstall.title: Remove the KUDO Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the KUDO release with the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall kudo --namespace kudo-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up KUDO CRDs
uninstall.description:
Delete the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) created by KUDO:
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep kudo.dev | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Remove the KUDO namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the `kudo-system` namespace to clean up any remaining resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kudo-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current KUDO state
upgrade.description:
Export the current state of KUDO resources before upgrading:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kudo-system -o yaml > kudo-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Make sure your Helm repository is up to date:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade KUDO to the latest version
upgrade.description:
Upgrade KUDO to the latest version with the following command:
```bash
helm upgrade kudo kudo/kudo --namespace kudo-system --version v0.19.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the KUDO pods to ensure they are running correctly after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kudo-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If KUDO pods are stuck in the Pending state, check for insufficient resources in your cluster:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n kudo-system
``` 
Ensure that your cluster has enough resources available or adjust the resource requests in the Helm chart.

troubleshooting.title: KUDO manager pod crashes
troubleshooting.description:
If the KUDO manager pod is crashing, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kudo-system
``` 
Look for any configuration issues or missing dependencies.

troubleshooting.title: RBAC issues preventing KUDO from functioning
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter permission errors, verify the RBAC configuration:
```bash
kubectl get clusterrolebinding kudo-role-binding -o yaml
``` 
Ensure that the service account has the necessary permissions.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KUDO will have all pods running in the `kudo-system` namespace, and you will be able to manage your applications using the KUDO framework. You can verify the installation by checking the pod statuses and ensuring that KUDO is configured correctly.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kudo https://mesosphere.github.io/charts/staging
helm repo update
helm install kudo kudo/kudo --namespace kudo-system --create-namespace --version v0.19.0
helm upgrade kudo kudo/kudo --namespace kudo-system --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi

kubectl create clusterrole kudo-role --verb=* --resource=* 

kubectl create clusterrolebinding kudo-role-binding --clusterrole=kudo-role --serviceaccount=kudo-system:kudo

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed.
